steps.title: Read the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x - 4)^2 + 5$ is in vertex form $a(x - h)^2 + k$. Matching: $h = 4$, $k = 5$. Vertex = $(4,\,5)$.

steps.title: Determine whether the vertex is a minimum or maximum
steps.content: The leading coefficient $a = \frac{1}{9} \gt 0$, so the parabola opens upward. The vertex is the minimum point of the graph.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context
steps.content: The vertex $(4,\,5)$ means: at $x = 4$ seconds, the car's height is $f(4) = 5$ inches — the minimum height on the track. That is the correct answer.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — The toy car's minimum height was 5 inches above the ground.: The vertex is $(4, 5)$ and $a \gt 0$ makes it a minimum; $f = 5$ inches is the minimum height. ✓
  • Choice B — The toy car's minimum height was 4 inches above the ground.: Confuses $h = 4$ (the time in seconds) with the height value; the height at the vertex is 5 inches, not 4. ✗
  • Choice C — The toy car's height was 5 inches above the ground when it started moving.: The car started at $x = 0$, giving $f(0) = \frac{1}{9}(0-4)^2 + 5 = \frac{16}{9} + 5 \approx 6.78$ inches, not 5 inches. ✗
  • Choice D — The toy car's height was 4 inches above the ground when it started moving.: Neither the starting height nor 4 as a height value is supported by the function. ✗
That is the correct answer.
